calendar.matters: Petition To Set Aside Judgment For Final Distribution And Order Terminating Trust For Extrinsic Fraud

Petition To Set Aside Judgment For Final Distribution And Order Terminating Trust For Extrinsic Fraud
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Continued To Aug-03-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kevin O'Brien for petitioners Elaine Jaymot and Susan Varela, who were also present.
Carroll Collins III for respondents Crystal Owyang and Jason Owyang, who were also present.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition to Set Aside Judgment for Final Distribution and Order Terminating Trust for Extrinsic Fraud.

The Court noted that a joint status report had not been filed as previously ordered. Mr. O'Brien apologized and reported that petitioners' appeal in the related Contra Costa matter remains pending. He stated the appellate record was certified February 18 and that the opening brief is due within 40 days, although extensions are anticipated. Mr. O'Brien estimated the appeal would likely take at least six months to resolve.

The Court observed the appeal is unlikely to be resolved in the near term and inquired whether mediation would be appropriate while the appeal is pending. Counsel indicated they would continue discussions but requested a continuance.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to August 3, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and ordered the parties to file a joint status report two weeks prior to that hearing.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Set Aside Judgment For Final Distribution And Order Terminating Trust For Extrinsic Fraud
Off Calendar Jun-02-2025 Continued To Mar-02-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Kevin O'Brien appeared via zoom for and with Elaine Jaymot and Susan Varela (petitioners).
Caroll Collins appeared via foe and with Crystal Owang and Jason Owyang (respodents).

Discussion was held regarding the motion and petitioners lost the motion hearing in Contra Costa Courts, that a notice of appeal was filed, that parties requested continuance for the resolution of the appeal. The matter is continued to March 2, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court instructed parties to file a joint status report 2 weeks prior to the hearing,

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Petition To Set Aside Judgment For Final Distribution And Order Terminating Trust For Extrinsic Fraud
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Continued To Jun-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Set Aside Judgment For Final Distribution And Order Terminating Trust For Extrinsic Fraud
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Continued To Apr-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ana Allec for Elaine Jaymot and Susan Varela, Petitioners (via Zoom).
Carroll Collins for Crystal Owyang and Jason Owyang, Objectors (via Zoom).

The Court inquired about the status of related proceedings in Contra Costa County. Mr. Collins reported that the related matter, along with four other matters, was scheduled for hearing on January 30, 2025. At that hearing, the Contra Costa County Court continued the five motions to an evidentiary hearing set for March 14, 2025, at 1:30 PM.
He further stated that if the settlement agreement at issue in that proceeding is enforced, it would resolve this case, making further proceedings in San Francisco unnecessary. Ms. Allec confirmed that she agreed with Mr. Collins' statements. Both parties jointly requested a continuance pending the Contra Costa County Court's ruling.

The Court continued the matter to April 28, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The parties were directed to submit a joint status update one week prior to the next hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Set Aside Judgment For Final Distribution And Order Terminating Trust For Extrinsic Fraud
Off Calendar Oct-28-2024 Continued To Feb-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kevin O'Brien, Esq., in court, for and with Elaine O. Jaymot and Susan Varela, Petitioners.
Carroll Collins, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Jason Owyang, and Crystal Owyang, Objectors.

At the outset, the court acknowledges the number of years since the original petition was filed and notes that the attorneys who originally represented the parties, as well as the notary public who purportedly acknowledged the signatures on the original trust, the main subject of the instant petition, are deceased. The court also mentions the recent settlement agreement. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the above. Mr. Collins reports on recent movement in this matter and confirms that there is a pending motion to enforce settlement agreement set for hearing in January, in Contra Costa County. There is additional discussion regarding the subject petition and how it makes sense to continue this matter to a date after the above-referenced motion.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Feb-24-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Belen. Not Reported.

Petition To Set Aside Judgment For Final Distribution And Order Terminating Trust For Extrinsic Fraud
